prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(register_record input_record_a1)
(register_record input_record_b1)
(register_record candidate_1)
(register_record candidate_2)
(assign_priority_slot input_record_a1 priority_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(confirm_record_eligibility input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(propose_bucket_a_primary input_record_a1 bucket_key_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_matcher_worker input_record_a1 matcher_worker_1)
(confirm_bucket_assignment_a input_record_a1 bucket_key_a1 matcher_worker_1)
(release_matcher_worker input_record_a1 matcher_worker_1)
(release_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_priority_slot input_record_b1 priority_slot_2)
(assign_attribute_slot input_record_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(confirm_record_eligibility input_record_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(propose_bucket_b_primary input_record_b1 bucket_key_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_matcher_worker input_record_b1 matcher_worker_1)
(confirm_bucket_assignment_b input_record_b1 bucket_key_b1 matcher_worker_1)
(release_matcher_worker input_record_b1 matcher_worker_1)
(release_attribute_slot input_record_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(allocate_container_primary_a_primary_b input_record_a1 input_record_b1 bucket_key_a1 bucket_key_b1 container_1)
(assign_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(activate_container container_1 input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(materialize_record_a input_record_a1 container_1)
(materialize_record_b input_record_b1 container_1)
(assign_priority_slot candidate_1 priority_slot_3)
(release_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot candidate_1 attribute_slot_1)
(confirm_record_eligibility candidate_1 attribute_slot_1)
(reserve_secondary_key candidate_1 secondary_key_1 container_1)
(mark_candidate_ready_for_activation candidate_1 secondary_key_1 container_1 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_attribute_priority_token candidate_1 attribute_priority_token_1)
(assign_matcher_worker candidate_1 matcher_worker_1)
(apply_attribute_priority_candidate_without_container_flag candidate_1 attribute_priority_token_1 matcher_worker_1 secondary_key_1 container_1)
(release_matcher_worker candidate_1 matcher_worker_1)
(release_attribute_priority_token candidate_1 attribute_priority_token_1)
(attach_finalizer_token candidate_1 finalizer_token_1)
(apply_tie_breaker candidate_1 finalizer_token_1 secondary_key_1 container_1)
(release_finalizer_token candidate_1 finalizer_token_1)
(select_canonical_candidate_initial candidate_1)
(release_attribute_slot candidate_1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_priority_marker_to_selected_record input_record_a1 priority_marker_1 attribute_slot_1)
(materialize_record_a_and_release_resources input_record_a1 priority_slot_1 priority_marker_1)
(assign_priority_slot candidate_2 priority_slot_1)
(release_attribute_slot input_record_a1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot candidate_2 attribute_slot_1)
(confirm_record_eligibility candidate_2 attribute_slot_1)
(reserve_secondary_key candidate_2 secondary_key_2 container_1)
(mark_candidate_ready_for_activation candidate_2 secondary_key_2 container_1 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_attribute_priority_token candidate_2 attribute_priority_token_1)
(assign_matcher_worker candidate_2 matcher_worker_1)
(apply_attribute_priority_candidate_without_container_flag candidate_2 attribute_priority_token_1 matcher_worker_1 secondary_key_2 container_1)
(release_matcher_worker candidate_2 matcher_worker_1)
(release_attribute_priority_token candidate_2 attribute_priority_token_1)
(attach_finalizer_token candidate_2 finalizer_token_1)
(apply_tie_breaker candidate_2 finalizer_token_1 secondary_key_2 container_1)
(release_finalizer_token candidate_2 finalizer_token_1)
(select_canonical_candidate_initial candidate_2)
(release_attribute_slot candidate_2 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot input_record_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_priority_marker_to_selected_record input_record_b1 priority_marker_1 attribute_slot_1)
(materialize_record_b_and_release_resources input_record_b1 priority_slot_2 priority_marker_1)
(release_attribute_slot input_record_b1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot candidate_1 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_priority_marker_to_selected_record candidate_1 priority_marker_1 attribute_slot_1)
(materialize_candidate_and_release_resources candidate_1 priority_slot_3 priority_marker_1)
(release_attribute_slot candidate_1 attribute_slot_1)
(assign_attribute_slot candidate_2 attribute_slot_1)
(attach_priority_marker_to_selected_record candidate_2 priority_marker_1 attribute_slot_1)
(materialize_candidate_and_release_resources candidate_2 priority_slot_1 priority_marker_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a planning problem in PDDL for record deduplication with priorities. I need to output only the sequence of ground actions that solve it, each on its own line in the form (action-name args...).


## Defining the Problem  
- The task involves solving a planning problem in PDDL for record deduplication with priorities.  
- The goal is to output a sequence of ground actions, each on its own line, in the format (action-name args...).  

## Outlining the Output  
- Each action must be a specific, grounded instance, not a general template.  
- The sequence should represent a valid solution to the deduplication problem, following the given priorities.

## Examining Record Types  
- All records—input_record_a1, input_record_b1, candidate_1, and candidate_2—are classified under record types, derived from record_root.  
- Input_record_group and merged_candidate_group are subtypes of record, confirming their record status.  

## Identifying Process Steps  
- Records must be registered, assigned priority and attribute slots, and confirmed eligible before further processing.  
- The process includes selecting markers, finalizing readiness, and materializing with resource release to achieve output_mark.  

## Noting Differences in Paths  
- Input records and merged candidates follow specific pipelines, with candidates already having candidate_assigned_container in the initial state.  
- Finalizer_token_1 is available, and tie_breaker_token may be needed for resolution.

## Mapping Record Processing  
- Input records like input_record_a1 require registration, priority slot assignment, and attribute slot assignment to progress.  
- The process involves confirming eligibility, proposing bucket assignments, and assigning matcher workers to set processed flags.  

## Managing Limited Resources  
- Only three priority slots (priority_slot_1, 2, 3) and one attribute slot are available, requiring sequential processing for four records.  
- Slots must be reused by releasing after materializing one record, then assigning to the next to achieve output_mark for all.  

## Sequencing Record Operations  
- Registration of all four records (input_record_a1, input_record_b1, candidate_1, candidate_2) is the initial step, with order not critical.  
- Processing involves careful sequencing to handle shared resources like container_1, already assigned to candidates, and ensure all reach output_mark.

## Sequencing Record Operations
- Processing input records like a1 and b1 requires registering, assigning priority and attribute slots, and confirming eligibility.
- To allocate a container, both a1 and b1 must be fully processed with input_*_processed_flag and record_*_confirmed set, requiring sequential handling due to limited slots.

## Managing Resource Conflicts
- Attribute_slot_1, shared by a1 and b1, must remain linked for activation, complicating sequential processing.
- Releasing resources like attribute slots and matcher workers after confirmation allows reuse, but timing is critical for container allocation.

## Exploring Container Allocation
- Allocate_container action requires both input records confirmed and container_reserved, using container_1 for primary paths.
- Activate_container needs the attribute link on one input, suggesting a need for careful planning to balance resource use.

## Analyzing Materialization Paths
- Materialize_record_a/b sets selected_marker, requiring container_reserved and activated, linking back to container allocation needs.
- Candidates use container_1 differently, for secondary keys, highlighting shared resource challenges in the process.

## Sequencing Record Operations  
- Processing input records like a1 and b1 involves registering, assigning priority and attribute slots, proposing primary buckets, and confirming assignments to set processed and confirmed flags.  
- Confirm_bucket_assignment sets both processed_flag and confirmed, which are required for container allocation, ensuring sequential processing due to limited resources.  

## Managing Resource Conflicts  
- After confirming assignments, resources like matcher workers and attribute slots must be released for reuse, but attribute_link is needed later for activate_container.  
- Releasing attribute_slot after processing creates a conflict, as activate_container requires attribute_link on one input record, necessitating re-assignment.  

## Exploring Container Allocation  
- Allocate_container requires both input records confirmed and sets container_reserved, but activate_container needs attribute_link, which was previously released.  
- Re-assigning attribute_slot to a previously processed input (like a1) allows activation, enabling materialization to set selected_marker.  

## Analyzing Materialization Paths  
- Materialize_record_a/b requires container_reserved, activated, and confirmation, setting selected_marker without needing attribute_link after activation.  
- The process allows materializing both a1 and b1 using container_1, balancing resource use despite earlier conflicts.

## Examining Candidate Processing  
- Applying tie_breaker sets tie_breaker_applied, requiring a finalizer_token attached beforehand with attach_finalizer_token.  
- Select_canonical_candidate_initial sets candidate_finalized and selected_marker after tie_breaker_applied, with no preference_feature_combined flags.  

## Managing Token Availability  
- Only one finalizer_token and attribute_priority_token exist, requiring sequential processing and releasing tokens after use for reuse.  
- Multiple secondary keys can be reserved for the same container, but priority slots are limited, needing release for new candidates.  

## Handling Resource Reuse  
- After processing, matcher workers and attribute slots can be released for reuse, with release_matcher_worker and release_attribute_slot available.  
- Attaching priority markers later requires re-linking attribute slots, as attr_link may be released during earlier steps.

## Finalizing Candidate Records  
- Records a1, b1, c1, and c2 all have selected_marker, with a1 already output_marked and slot1 released.  
- Finalizing b1, c1, and c2 involves re-assigning attribute slots, attaching priority markers, and materializing records, ensuring resource availability.  

## Managing Attribute Slot Reuse  
- Attribute slots are released after each record’s materialization, allowing reuse for subsequent candidates like c1 and c2.  
- Process involves linking slots to records, attaching markers, and releasing resources to maintain availability for finalization.  

## Ensuring Processing Order  
- Candidates c1 and c2 require registration and eligibility checks before assigning priority slots, ensuring orderly processing.  
- Tokens and slots must be released from prior candidates (e.g., c1) before finalizing subsequent ones (e.g., c2) to avoid conflicts.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
